name: HQ.ai Option 3 - Ink & Amber (Mercury/Linear-influenced precision dark)
intent: Sub-brand identity. Dark-first like Linear/Mercury, with a single warm-amber accent that signals action and encodes the Australian context (sun, ochre, FWA-safe). Best fit for SMB owners who use HQ.ai daily as an operations tool not a marketing site - dashboards, transcripts, recruitment review.
rationaleVsCurrent: Current Option 1 has zero dark-mode story. Option 3 inverts the assumption: HQ.ai is software you spend hours in, not a homepage you visit. Mercury and Linear prove dark+single-accent is the highest-trust option for daily-driver SaaS. Amber accent (vs Mercury blue / Linear violet) avoids fintech/devtool clichés and has natural Australian resonance.

amber.role: primary CTA, focus ring, active nav

amberSoft.role: highlights, badge bg (used sparingly on light surfaces only)

warning.note: shares amber to avoid confusion - reserve red strictly for destructive

family.sans: Geist Variable, Inter Variable, system-ui, sans-serif
family.mono: Geist Mono, JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, monospace

shadow.principle: Mercury/Anthropic discipline - shadow used ONLY for floating overlays. Cards use border + bg lift instead.

wcag.tertiary_on_bg: 3.4:1 - AA Large only - never use for body

implementationNotes: Default theme = dark, with .light opt-in (inverts the current model)
Sidebar already dark in current build - this just extends it everywhere
DOCX/PDF outputs stay light-mode regardless of UI mode (don't tint generated documents)
Amber CTA must always meet 44px min touch target with bold or semibold label
Reserve amberSoft for badges on LIGHT surfaces only - poor contrast on dark
